feed.title: 🏡 Seller Tip of the Day:…
caps: linkedin

🏡 Seller Tip of the Day:
One of the biggest mistakes I see sellers make is thinking that higher is always better when it comes to pricing. The truth? Buyers are watching the market closely, and an overpriced home can end up costing you time and money.
The goal isn't to list high—it's to list smart. A strategic price can create demand, attract serious buyers, and help you get the best possible outcome.
Would you rather price your home based on what you hope it's worth or what the market is willing to pay? 👇

Delays cost time AND money. If your draw process is holding up your projects, it is time for a serious upgrade. 

I always recommend Sitewire to my mentees and fellow flippers because it turns a week-long wait into a same-day turnaround.

Watching my mentees use it out in the field, the process is an absolute no-brainer: 
1️⃣ Borrower submits proof of work right from the job site. 
2️⃣ An expert GC verifies the work remotely. 
3️⃣ The lender approves the draw via their dashboard. 
4️⃣ The funds get transferred same-day!
If your lender is still making you wait days for an inspector to show up, you’re losing money. 

Take control of your projects and tell your lender to look into Sitewire! Let’s get these deals closed faster! 🚀

💡 Myth or Fact Question of the Day 💡
Many homeowners assume that a cash offer is automatically the best offer. While cash can offer certain advantages, experienced sellers know there's more to consider than just how the buyer is paying. 👀
The strongest offer often comes down to the complete package: closing timeline, contingencies, proof of funds, flexibility, and the buyer's ability to actually get to the closing table.
That's why smart sellers don't just look at the price or whether it's cash—they evaluate the entire offer before making a decision. 🏡
Were you surprised by this fact?

“How do I get the money?” 💰
I get this question all the time. Here’s the truth:
Cash works, but it limits you. You can only use what you have.
Private money changes the game. That’s someone in your circle, a family member, a friend, someone from church, lending you what you need. You agree on the terms together, privately. You usually need about six months: buy it, renovate it, sell it, pay them back.
Most people think it’s bank loans or nothing. It’s not. I’ve done this with private money over and over, and I teach my mentees how to do the same.

“How do I get the money?” 💰
I get this question all the time. Here’s the truth:
Cash works, but it limits you. You can only use what you have.
Private money changes the game. That’s someone in your circle, a family member, a friend, someone from church, lending you what you need. You agree on the terms together, privately. You usually need about six months: buy it, renovate it, sell it, pay them back.
Most people think it’s bank loans or nothing. It’s not. I’ve done this with private money over and over, and I teach my mentees how to do the same.

feed.title: "Being pre-qualified gives you the same…
caps: linkedin

"Being pre-qualified gives you the same advantage as being pre-approved when making an offer."
Many buyers use these terms interchangeably, but understanding the difference can have a major impact on your home-buying journey. 👀
A pre-qualification is typically a quick estimate of what you may be able to afford based on information you provide. A pre-approval, on the other hand, involves a deeper review of your finances by a lender and shows sellers that you're a more serious and prepared buyer.
In a competitive market, that extra level of preparation can make a big difference when it's time to submit an offer. 💪🏡
So, what's your answer: Myth or Fact? Drop it in the comments before revealing the answer! ⬇️

feed.title: 7 rules before you make your…
caps: instagram

7 rules before you make your first real estate investment 🏡

When I was still a principal, nobody handed me a roadmap. I had to figure out what to do — and more importantly, what NOT to do — before I put a single dollar into a deal.

Here’s what I wish someone told me first 👇
1️⃣ Don’t wait until you’re “ready” — the perfect time doesn’t exist

2️⃣ Don’t invest in a market just because you live there — follow the data

3️⃣ Know your strategy BEFORE you look at properties — pick your lane first

4️⃣ Run the numbers before you fall in love — cash flow doesn’t care about the kitchen

5️⃣ Know your financing options before you make an offer — or you’ll lose the deal

6️⃣ Build your team before you need them — agent, lender, contractor on speed dial

7️⃣ Have the hard money conversation with yourself — how much can you actually invest?

Most people skip straight to browsing Zillow.

Then wonder why nothing works out. ✋🏽
The investors who win? 

They do the boring work BEFORE the deal shows up.

Ready to stop guessing and get a real strategy mapped out for YOUR first investment?

feed.title: 🏡 MYTH OR FACT 🏡 "The…
caps: linkedin

🏡 MYTH OR FACT 🏡
"The property with the highest cap rate is always the best investment."
This is one of those questions that separates investors who know the numbers from investors who know the business. 👀
A high cap rate might look great at first glance, but experienced investors know that's only one piece of the puzzle. The best deals aren't always the ones with the biggest returns on paper—they're the ones that align with your goals, market conditions, risk tolerance, and long-term strategy.
Before you invest, ask yourself:
✔️ Is the property in a strong market?
✔️ What are the vacancy rates?
✔️ Is there appreciation potential?
✔️ What are the maintenance and management costs?
✔️ Does it fit my overall portfolio strategy?
The most successful investors look beyond a single metric and analyze the complete picture. 📈🏡
So, what's your answer: Myth or Fact? Drop it in the comments before swiping for the answer! ⬇️

caps: linkedin

🏡 MYTH OR FACT 🏡
“Accepting a cash offer is always the fastest and safest way to sell a home.”
A lot of sellers assume that “cash” automatically means no risk and the quickest closing—but that’s not always the full story. 👀
While cash offers can eliminate loan-related delays, the strength of the deal still depends on other important factors like proof of funds, buyer credibility, contingencies, closing timeline, and whether the buyer is truly ready and able to perform.
A smart seller doesn’t just look at HOW the buyer is paying—they evaluate the full offer. Because the best deal isn’t just fast… it’s also secure and reliable all the way to closing day. 🏡🔑

caps: linkedin

🏡 MYTH OR FACT 🏡
"Offering over the asking price automatically means you'll win in a multiple-offer situation."
It's easy to think that the highest offer always wins—but experienced buyers know that's only part of the equation. 💡
When sellers review multiple offers, they're looking at the entire package—not just the purchase price. Financing strength, pre-approval, contingencies, earnest money, flexibility on the closing date, and the overall likelihood of a smooth transaction can all influence their decision.
That's why a well-structured offer can sometimes beat a higher-priced one.
The key isn't just offering more—it's offering smarter. As a buyer, understanding what matters most to the seller can give you a competitive edge without stretching beyond your budget.

feed.title: Thinking about flipping houses? Here is…
caps: youtube

Thinking about flipping houses? Here is a lesson every new investor needs to hear before they hire their first contractor. Coaches Jenn and Jose broke this down at our Principles of Real Estate cohort kickoff, and it is one of the most practical pieces of advice in the program.

The moment you stop showing up to your renovation site, the pace of work can slow down or shift entirely. Your GC might tell you they are handling the roof today, but if you are not there to verify, you might come back to find they were still on framing. That gap between what is promised and what is done is where renovations go over budget and over schedule.

Jenn's approach is simple. She visits her active renovation properties every single day when she can. For investors still working a nine to five, she recommends at minimum driving by to confirm the site is active and the right work is happening.

feed.title: The BRRRR method is still one…
caps: youtube

The BRRRR method is still one of the most effective ways to build a rental portfolio, but high interest rates have changed the math. In this clip from a live Principles of Real Estate class, Deba Douglas explains exactly how she acquires rentals today and what adjustments her mentees need to make right now.

Deba's approach: buy distressed, rehab, place a tenant, and do a cash-out refinance to recover your capital. In past markets, you could pull back most of what you put in. With DSCR loan rates around 7.25% and rising insurance and tax costs, that refinance may return only half, or less.

So what is the smart move in today's market? Buy at 60 to 65% of the ARV. That's the number that keeps your deal alive when financing is expensive.

This is not theory. This is what Deba teaches from her own rental portfolio, built from the ground up, starting long before real estate felt accessible to her.

If you are a W-2 worker trying to figure out where to start with rentals, or you are already looking at deals and need help running the numbers, this video is your starting point.

feed.title: 🏡 MYTH OR FACT 🏡 "An…
caps: linkedin

🏡 MYTH OR FACT 🏡
"An investor can lose money on a property even if the property appreciates in value."
Many people believe that if a property's value goes up, the investment is automatically a success. But experienced investors know the real answer is much more nuanced. 👀
Appreciation builds wealth over time, but it doesn't guarantee profitability. If you're losing money every month to negative cash flow, facing costly repairs, dealing with extended vacancies, or paying high financing and holding costs, a rising property value may not be enough to make the investment worthwhile.
That's why successful investors don't chase appreciation alone—they analyze the entire deal. They look at cash flow, expenses, financing, market trends, and their exit strategy before ever making an offer.
Remember: You make your money when you buy, protect it while you own, and realize it when you sell. 📈🏡
So, what's your answer—Myth or Fact? Let me know in the comments before revealing the answer! ⬇️

One of the most important lessons in real estate investing sounds simple. But most people skip it.

The driveway lies.

I was walking a property recently when the neighbor pulled up in an immaculate Mercedes. My brain immediately made assumptions. Then she opened the door and a bowl of cat food slid across the front seat.

And I thought, there it is. That's the lesson.

In real estate, the outside of a property, or what's parked in front of it, tells you almost nothing about the deal inside. I have walked into beautifully landscaped homes with significant structural damage hiding behind the walls. I have walked into rough-looking properties that turned out to be some of the strongest investment opportunities I've seen.

Curb appeal is not due diligence.

Every investor, whether they are just starting out or building their tenth deal, needs to develop the habit of getting inside. Walking the property. Asking the right questions. Seeing past the surface.
